column2: A process calls send(buf, dest) and the call returns immediately — without waiting for the receiver to call recv. Which statement is most likely true?
column3: The message has been delivered to the receiver.
column4: The message has been copied into an OS kernel buffer; the process buffer can be reused.
column5: The receiver has acknowledged the message.
column6: The kernel will notify the process later via a signal when the buffer is free.

column2: A client uses non-blocking receive and busy-polls in a loop waiting for a reply. What is the main cost of this approach compared to blocking receive? Name one scenario where you would deliberately choose non-blocking receive.
A distributed system designer states: 'Any message from node A to node B arrives within 100 ms.' Which timing model does this describe?
Without a neutral wire format, how many pairwise translation functions are needed between n programming languages in the worst case?
column5: O(n) — linear in the number of languages
column6: O(n^2) — quadratic in the number of languages

column2: What does Protocol Buffers (.proto + protoc) give you that Python msgpack does NOT? Select all that apply.
column3: Type checking at compile time — invalid field names are caught before running the program.
column4: The ability to serialize data at all.
column5: Language-neutral code generation (e.g. one endpoint in Go; another in Java).
column6: Smaller wire-format size in all cases.

column2: A client reads a 10 MB file from a server in 1 MB chunks; the server remembers the file offset between calls. Which statement is true?
column3: This is a stateless server because the client carries no state.
column4: This is a stateful server; if it crashes and restarts the next read may fail or return wrong data.
column5: This is a stateless server because nothing is stored permanently on disk.
column6: Stateful vs. stateless has no impact on fault tolerance.

column2: A client sends a request to transfer 100 EUR from account A to B. The server executes the transfer but the reply is lost. The client times out and retransmits. What is the risk if the server does not track processed message IDs?
column3: The client will never receive a reply.
column4: 200 EUR may be transferred instead of 100 EUR.
column5: The transfer may be automatically rolled back.
column6: Nothing — the server detects duplicates automatically.

column2: Which disadvantage of the basic two-tier client/server architecture is directly identified in the lecture?
column4: The server can become a performance bottleneck and a single point of failure.
column5: Clients cannot communicate with each other.
column6: The wire format becomes too complex.
